title: ES Modules (ESM)
body:
## Definition

**ES modules** are the standard `import`/`export` syntax for JavaScript modules — statically analyzable and natively supported in browsers and Node.

## Why it exists

Replace ad-hoc script tags and CommonJS for tree-shakeable, async-loadable modules.

## How it works

```js
import { fn } from "./utils.js";
export const x = 1;
```

`import` is hoisted; live bindings for exports. CJS `require` is synchronous legacy in Node.

## In practice

All modern bundlers and [Vite](concept:vite) dev server rely on ESM. `"type": "module"` in package.json.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

**[ES modules](concept:es-modules)** are the standard `import`/`export` syntax for JavaScript modules — statically analyzable and natively supported in browsers and Node.

## Why it exists

Replace ad-[hoc](concept:higher-order-component) script tags and CommonJS for tree-shakeable, async-loadable modules.

## How it works

```js
import { fn } from "./utils.js";
export const x = 1;
```

`import` is [hoisted](concept:hoisting); live [bindings](concept:binding) for exports. CJS `require` is synchronous legacy in Node.

## In practice

All modern [bundlers](concept:module-bundlers) and [Vite](concept:vite) dev server rely on [ESM](concept:es-modules). `"type": "module"` in package.json.
